assessment_history.summary: The "workspace_entity" PRD is well-structured, detailed, and complete across all required and recommended sections. The Intent is clear and concise, Goals are concrete and implementation-tied, Non-Goals explicitly bound the scope, and Background provides strong context and motivation. The document goes well beyond a typical PRD by including schema definitions, REST endpoint contracts (request/response/error tables), CLI command specifications, architecture update requirements, dependency tracking, tech stack details, and documented design decisions with rationale. It is ready to proceed to artifact generation without requiring further clarification.
gaps: The 'owner' field in the YAML front-matter is empty — the responsible owner/team for this spec is unspecified, which may be a tracking concern but does not block implementation.
There is no explicit mention of test coverage expectations (unit, integration, or end-to-end tests) for the new endpoint, store, or CLI command.

The PRD does not specify whether the slug uniqueness constraint is global or scoped (e.g., per-team or per-user). Based on the schema (UNIQUE on slug), it appears to be global, but this could cause confusion for users expecting team-scoped slugs.
No explicit mention of how the CLI authenticates (e.g., config file, environment variable for API key) — this may be handled elsewhere but is not referenced.

The architecture document update requirement (section 'Architecture Document Updates') is described qualitatively but lacks a checklist of specific files to be modified beyond the two named documents.

questions.text: Who is the owner or responsible team for this spec?
questions.context: The 'owner' field in the YAML front-matter is blank. Knowing the owner ensures accountability and correct routing for reviews and sign-offs.

questions.text: Are there explicit test coverage requirements for this spec (e.g., unit tests for the store, integration tests for the REST endpoint, CLI smoke tests)?
questions.context: The PRD does not mention testing expectations. Defining this upfront avoids ambiguity during implementation and review.
options: Unit + integration tests for the REST endpoint
No specific requirement — follow project conventions

questions.text: Is workspace slug uniqueness intended to be global (across all users and teams) or scoped (e.g., per-team or per-user)?
questions.context: The schema defines slug as globally UNIQUE. If users expect to reuse the same slug in different teams (e.g., 'my-api' in team A and team B), a global constraint would block that. Clarifying this prevents future migration pain.
options: Global uniqueness (current schema — slug is unique across the entire system)
Scoped uniqueness (e.g., unique per team or per user — would require schema change)

questions.text: Which specific files in the architecture/ and docs/ directories need to be updated as part of the architecture alignment requirement?
questions.context: The PRD names 'architecture/services-architecture.md' explicitly but refers to other documents generically. A concrete file list would make the implementation task unambiguous.
